insight:
Yesterday was a solid recovery day with your body showing great signs heading into race week! With no formal training logged and only 4,619 steps, you gave your legs the rest they needed. Your 2,323 calories burned hit right on target, while your low stress level of 27/100 and excellent resting heart rate of 44 bpm show you're in prime condition.

Your HRV of 113ms sits beautifully in your optimal range, and combined with that stellar resting HR, your body is telling us it's recovered and race-ready. However, that 6.6 hours of sleep is cutting it close for race prep - your body does its final adaptations during deep sleep phases.

Today, prioritize getting 7-8 hours of sleep tonight since it's your final full night before race day. Your recovery markers are spot-on, so stick to your planned easy activities, but make sleep your performance priority. Consider an earlier bedtime and limiting screens after 9 PM.
